data.title: Wetherspoon Posts Rise in Profit But Warns of Higher Costs
data.description:
By Cristina Gallardo

J.D. Wetherspoon reported a jump in pretax profit for the year, but warned cost increases stemming from U.K. government policies on energy and workers' pay will weigh on its results.

The London-listed pub chain said Friday that pretax profit rose 10% to 81.4 million pounds ($109.4 million) in the 52 weeks to July 27 compared with the same period of last year.

Revenue grew 4.5% to 2.13 billion pounds, while like-for-like sales increased 5.1%.

The company didn't provide forecasts for the current fiscal year, but said it expects a "reasonable outcome".

However, higher employer contributions to the U.K. national insurance payroll tax and an increase to the minimum wage will push up costs by about 60 million pounds a year, Wetherspoon said. Meanwhile, the introduction of new energy levies will add 7 million pounds to costs, it said.

Wetherspoon said it plans to open about 15 managed pubs this fiscal year. It currently operates 794 pubs.

Write to Cristina Gallardo at cristina.gallardo@wsj.com

data.title: Clariant's Additional Lawsuits Will Weigh on Sentiment — Market Talk
data.description: Clariant's stock faces headwinds as it receives lawsuits number 7 and 8, which allege its purchases of ethylene broke European Union competition law, Vontobel analyst Sibylle Bischofberger writes. Friday's announcement that BP is alleging damages totaling 1.1 billion euros and ExxonMobil is alleging 860 million euros in damages is unexpected because the deadline for filing claims was thought to be in May, Bischofberger says. The additional cases mean the Swiss chemical company faces claims totaling 8.4 billion euros and this will weigh on sentiment, Bischofberger says. Shares closed at 7.41 Swiss francs Thursday. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

data.title: UK Stocks-Factors to watch on October 3
data.description:
Britain's FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 index is seen opening higher on Friday, with futures up 0.13%.

* BEAUTY TECH GROUP: Beauty Tech Group (IPO-BEAU.L) has priced its IPO at 271 pence per share, implying a market capitalisation of 300 million pounds ($402.90 million), as the British company prepares to make its London market debut on Friday.

* J D WETHERSPOON : - British pub group J D Wetherspoon reported a more than 10% rise in annual adjusted pretax profit, helped by resilient demand for its budget-friendly drinks and pub grub.

* BP: BP said it will seek a partner for its major Bumerangue oil discovery in Brazil and could close a deal before making a final investment decision on the project

* SHELL: Shell-led LNG Canada has begun starting up its second 6.5 million tonnes per annum (mtpa) liquefied natural gas processing unit, Train 2, in Kitimat, British Columbia, a spokesperson told Reuters.

* SHAWBROOK : British bank Shawbrook could file for an initial public offering as early as the coming days, two people familiar with the matter said.

* ZEGONA: Zegona Communications is mulling a potential sale of the five data centers it acquired through its purchase of Vodafone's Spanish unit, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

* COMMODITIES: Oil prices rose slightly but were on track for their steepest weekly decline since late June, copper prices rose on supply disruptions and gold held steady, buoyed by expectations of further U.S. interest rate cuts.

* For more on the factors affecting European stocks, please click on:

data.title: Asahi Cyberattack Stokes Fear Japan Could Run Dry of Its Favorite Beer — Analysis
data.description:
By Fabiana Negrin Ochoa and Megumi Fujikawa

Supply of Japan's most popular beer could be in danger of drying up.

Four days after Asahi Group was targeted by cyberattackers, output at many of the brewer's plants in Japan remains offline. The company isn't taking new orders and shipments to customers have been delayed. Management this week took the decision to postpone the launch of a dozen new products planned for October.

That is a setback for the company that owns beer brands including Asahi Super Dry, Italy's Peroni and Pilsner Urquell from the Czech Republic. It also makes popular soft drinks such as Calpico and Mitsuya Cider.

Just how costly of a setback depends on when production can resume after the cyberattack that caused a system failure.

"If they resolve it in a short space of time, then it's going to be completely negligible. If this drags on for a month, then it'll be somewhat impactful. If it starts to drag on until December, then that's going to be a big issue," Euan McLeish, APAC food & beverages senior analyst at Bernstein.

Morgan Stanley MUFG analysts estimate the risk of losses at 1.5 billion to 2.0 billion yen a day under full suspension, or about $10.2 million to $13.6 million. Retail sales are likely unscathed for now, "but a prolonged stoppage would increase risk of retailers/consumers switching to rival products," they wrote in a note.

Asahi on Friday said it doesn't know when its supply chain will be restored to full health. It has taken some orders manually for a limited selection of products, including Super Dry, calling such action "an emergency measure."

"Although the production systems at our factories have not been affected by the cyberattack, production has been suspended at many Asahi Group factories in Japan due to the inability to ship goods," the company said.

Founded in 1889, the food-and-beverage giant operates about 30 production sites in Japan. Overseas supply appear to be unaffected for now.

The brewery leads a concentrated but shrinking Japanese beer market with a nearly 40% market share, according to Morningstar estimates, ahead of competitors such as Kirin, Suntory and Sapporo.

Shares of Asahi were hit this week, sliding 6.5%, but saying that the stock sold off significantly because of the cyberattack is a hard conclusion to draw, said Bernstein's McLeish.

The fall in its shares was in line with the decline of the broader food-and-beverage sector in Japan. Other beer makers weakened this week too, with Orion, Kirin and Sapporo shedding between 4.2% to 11%.

"Investors are not panicking at this stage," said McLeish.

He's not too worried that consumers' taste for Super Dry will sour, or that vendors will switch to another beer.

"The reason I've got confidence in this being a sort of temporal impact, is that Asahi Super Dry is by far the strongest beer brand in Japan, like by a long way," McLeish said.

Fans of the beer are unlikely to find store shelves empty just yet. Various 7-Eleven and FamilyMart convenience stores in Tokyo and Osaka were stocked with Asahi Super Dry on Friday afternoon.

"It's going to be more patchy in the restaurant channel, the izakayas--small customers who have very low inventories," said McLeish.

But even if some temporarily switch to another beer, Super Dry demand will snap back, he added.

Kazuhiro Sasaki, head of research at Phillip Securities Japan, agreed, noting that Asahi "products possess strong brand power. Even if items temporarily disappear from store shelves, they are expected to be easily restored once systems are back online."

"The impact [of the cyberattack] on [Asahi's] performance is viewed as a temporary factor and is not considered significant", Sasaki added.

The timing of the cyberattack could also be a bit of a silver lining, McLeish said.

October is one of the slowest months of the year for the sector. "Things generally calm down at this part of the year, and then they ramp up again in December, when you get into the holiday season. So it's probably quite a fortunate time for it to happen, if it's got to happen."

  • Jason Chau contributed to this report

Write to Fabiana Negrin Ochoa at fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com and Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: Balco Group appoints Andreas Lindberg as Director of Business Development
data.description:
Balco Group AB

Balco Group has appointed Andreas Lindberg as Director of Business Development. He will assume the position on November 1, 2025, and will at the same time join the Group Management Team. Andreas will also be responsible for the Group’s IT.

Andreas brings extensive experience from senior leadership roles in industry, sales, and business development. Most recently, he served as Managing Director of Wilo Nordic, where he successfully drove profitable growth in the Nordic organization. He has previously held senior management positions at ProfilGruppen, CTC Norway, and Sapa.

“Andreas combines a strategic mindset with solid operational experience. His ability to build organizations and develop new offerings will play an important role in strengthening Balco Group’s market position and creating additional value for our customers,” says Camilla Ekdahl, CEO of Balco Group.

For more information, please contact:

Camilla Ekdahl

CEO Balco Group

Email: camilla.ekdahl@balco.se

Mobile: +46 706 06 30 32

Jesper Magnusson

Head of HR & Communications

Email: jesper.magnusson@balco.se

Mobile: +46 733 456 141

About Balco Group

Balco Group is a leading player in the balcony industry, focused on providing innovative, patented and energy-efficient solutions for multi-dwelling buildings. The company’s tailored products contribute to enhancing quality of life, safety and the value of housing. Through a decentralized and efficient sales process, Balco Group maintains full control of its value chain – from production to delivery. Founded in 1987 in Växjö, the company today employs approximately 550 people and is the market leader in Scandinavia, with operations in several markets in Northern Europe. The company’s revenue for 2024 was 1,418 MSEK, and Balco Group has been listed on Nasdaq Stockholm since 2017.

data.title: J D Wetherspoon reports higher annual profit on resilient demand
data.description:
British pub group J D Wetherspoon reported a more than 10% rise in annual adjusted pretax profit on Friday, helped by resilient demand for its budget-friendly drinks and pub grub.

After a post-pandemic recovery in sales, the British hospitality sector is under renewed pressure as high inflation and rising living costs threaten to curb consumer spending.

The upcoming budget in November has also raised fears of potential tax hikes, adding to cost burdens from higher wages and national insurance contributions.

"Cost increases such as these will undoubtedly add to underlying inflation in the UK economy, although Wetherspoon, as always, will endeavour to keep price increases to a minimum," Chairman Tim Martin said in a statement.

However, customers continue to flock to the pub group, drawn by its low-price model as they try to rein in spending.

The company said it expects a "reasonable outcome" for its current financial year, though rising government-imposed costs, including energy, could affect performance.

Total sales in fiscal 2025 rose 4.5% from the prior year to 2.13 billion pounds ($2.86 billion), it said.

Before separately disclosed items, the company reported an adjusted pre-tax profit of 81.4 million pounds for the year ended July 27, compared with 73.9 million pounds last year.

($1 = 0.7444 pounds)

data.title: Europe before the bell: Stocks set for best week since April
data.description:
EUROPE BEFORE THE BELL: STOCKS SET FOR BEST WEEK SINCE APRIL

European stock futures are trading higher on Friday as investors continue to overlook the U.S. government shutdown and instead focus on the Fed, which is expected to lower interest rates again later this month.

Euro STOXX 50 futures are up 0.2%, as are STOXX 600 futures (FXXPc1). The STOXX 600 has already risen 2.4% this week, its biggest weekly jump since April.

"We remain in the camp that even if the shutdown extends for more than a few days, the market impact should be limited," says Jefferies economist Mohit Kumar in a note.

"Any dips should be a buying opportunity."

Futures on the FTSE , CAC (FCEc1) and DAX are up 0.1%-0.3%.

In corporate news, British pub chain J D Wetherspoon reported a more than 10% rise in annual profit, helped by resilient demand.

(Samuel Indyk)

*****

EARLIER ON LIVE MARKETS:

US POLITICS CAN'T SHUT DOWN THE ANIMAL SPIRITS CLICK HERE

data.title: Finland lacks jurisdiction in Baltic Sea cable breach case, court says
data.description: A Finnish district court ruled on Friday that Finland does not have jurisdiction to prosecute the captain and two officers of the Eagle S oil tanker, who were accused of breaking undersea power and internet cables in the Baltic Sea.

data.title: ZAWYA: UAE non-oil sector sees strongest growth in seven months: PMI
data.description:
First published: 03-Oct-2025 10:32:38

Staff Writer

The United Arab Emirates' non-oil private sector recorded its strongest expansion in seven months in September, driven by a sharp rise in new business inflows, according to a survey released on Friday.

The seasonally adjusted S&P Global UAE Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) rose to 54.2 in September from 53.3 in August, reflecting a robust recovery in demand.

Although the headline index remains below the survey’s long-run average, the latest reading suggests that non-oil business performance has rebounded well since its low point in July, said David Owen, Senior Economist at S&P Global Market Intelligence.

"Over 30% of surveyed firms reported an increase in new order intakes during the month, which drove a sharp improvement in sales growth from its over four-year low in August. The uplift pointed to some positive momentum in the domestic market as export sales growth remained relatively muted,” he said.

The improvement in sales volumes was largely supported by domestic demand, though new orders from foreign clients also saw an uptick.

Purchasing activity increased in September after declining for the first time in over four years in August. However, the report noted that the rise was relatively subdued compared to recent trends, as businesses cited sufficient stock levels, competitive pressures, and pricing concerns as factors limiting buying activity.

Employment levels rose at the fastest pace since May, though the increase remained modest.

Looking ahead, firms remained optimistic about the year ahead, albeit slightly less so than in August, which marked a ten-month high in sentiment.

Dubai keeps pace 

Non-oil companies in Dubai also saw a notable boost in sales at the end of the third quarter. The Dubai PMI climbed to 54.2 in September from 53.6 in August, mirroring the broader UAE trend. 

Firms reported a sharper increase in new work compared to August, with the pace of sales growth accelerating to an eight-month high, following only a modest upturn the previous month.

 (Writing by Brinda Darasha; editing by Daniel Luiz)  

brinda.darasha@lseg.com  

Disclaimer: This article is provided for informational purposes only. The content does not provide tax, legal or investment advice or opinion regarding the suitability, value or profitability of any particular security, portfolio or investment strategy. Read our full disclaimer policy here.

data.title: Nifty holds above 24,800, Sensex sees directionless trade while metals stage sharp rally
data.description:
Dalal Street saw range-bound trade in the afternoon session after trimming early losses on Friday, October 3, poised to end the holiday-shortened week on a positive note.

At 12 noon, the Sensex was down 4.97 points or 0.01 percent at 80,978.34, and the Nifty was down 9.45 points or 0.04 percent at 24,826.85. About 2147 shares advanced, 1459 shares declined, and 151 shares unchanged.

On the sectoral front, metal stocks shone bright. The Nifty Metal index rose nearly two percent, lifted by strong gains in Tata Steel, National Aluminium Company and Hindalco shares. The advance came as global cues turned supportive for capital-intensive sectors such as steel.

While the Bank Nifty index was flat, the Nifty Private Bank and Nifty PSU Bank indices saw some positive momentum. On the flip side, FMCG, auto, and pharma stocks were in the trade, seeing some selling pressure.

The positive impact of the central bank's bold initiatives to boost credit growth in the economy has the potential to sustain the momentum in the market, particularly in Bank Nifty. But this momentum is unlikely to sustain in the context of the sustained FII selling in the market.

According to VK Vijayakumar, Chief Investment Strategist, Geojit Investments, FIIs are likely to further accelerate selling since the market construct provides them the opportunity to sell aggressively. He added, "The huge short position in the market indicates that the bulls will be on the defensive. Aggressive DII buying can provide some support to the market particularly in largecap auto stocks, which have strong fundamental support now."

"From a technical perspective, a sustained move above 24,900 could pave the way for a rally toward 25,000 and 25,150. On the downside, immediate support is placed at 24,750 and 24,600, which may act as potential entry points for long trades," said Amruta Shinde, Technical & Derivative Analyst at Choice Equity Broking.

Follow our market blog to catch all the live updatesDisclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: CCPs are eligible to become monetary policy counterparties
data.description:
3 October 2025

Danmarks Nationalbank will allow central counterparty clearing houses (CCPs) offering clearing services in Danish kroner to become monetary policy counterparties (MPCs).

As MPCs, CCPs will be able to access to monetary policy instruments under the same terms as existing MPCs.

With these instruments, CCP's will more effectively be able to manage the collateral they receive as margin on cleared trades in their role as intermediaries in key Danish markets. This in turn, will support the effectiveness of krone money markets and monetary policy transmission.

This decision is consistent with actions taken by other central banks, which have also granted CCPs access to monetary policy instruments.

To be eligible to become an MPC, CCPs must be active in clearing transactions denominated in Danish kroner, and in the assessment of Danmarks Nationalbank, hold material significance for markets in Danish kroner considered material to the transmission of monetary policy. CCPs not domiciled in Denmark must be established and supervised in another EU member state.

CCPs interested in becoming monetary policy counterparties must also have an account in TARGET DKK and meet specific technical and operational requirements. Terms and Conditions for Account Holders contains a detailed description of the applicable rules for different types of participants, as well as the necessary forms for account holders and their administrators.

CCPs interested in applying can contact payments@nationalbanken.dk to discuss the terms and conditions in more detail.

Applications will be accepted from 6 October 2025.

Enquiries can be directed to press advisor Peter Levring on tel. +45 2620 1809.

.

Karin Tyche, Chief Investor Relations Officer

ir@hoistfinance.com

About Hoist Finance

Hoist Finance is an asset manager specialised in non-performing loans. For more than 30 years, we have invested in and managed debt portfolios. We are a partner to international banks and financial institutions across Europe, acquiring their non-performing loan portfolios. We are also a partner to consumers and smaller companies in a debt situation, creating long-term sustainable repayment plans enabling them to honour their commitments. We are a regulated credit market company under the supervision of the SFSA, present in 14 European countries. Our share is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm. data.title: Nordnet: Monthly statistics September
data.description:
Nordnet

In September, Nordnet’s customers made 5,444,100 trades in listed financial instruments, which corresponds to 247,600 trades per day. New customers in September amounted to 26,200. The annual customer growth rate is 12.9 percent adjusted for the divestment of Nordnet’s unsecured lending portfolio to Ikano Bank on October 1, 2024. The total number of customers at the end of September was 2,292,400. Net savings for September was SEK 5.9 billion and the total net savings during 2025 is SEK 60.2 billion. The savings capital was SEK 1,142 billion and lending amounted to SEK 28.4 billion at the end of September.

5 Average number of trades per day is calculated as total trades per market divided by the number of days each stock exchange was open.

For more information, please contact:

Johan Tidestad, Chief Communications Officer

johan.tidestad@nordnet.se, +46 708 875 775

Marcus Lindberg, Head of Investor Relations

marcus.lindberg@nordnet.se, +46 764 923 128

Nordnet is a pan-Nordic leading digital platform for savings and investments. Through innovation, simplicity and transparency, we challenge traditional structures, and give private savers access to the same information, tools and services as professionals. Visit us at www.nordnetab.com, www.nordnet.se, www.nordnet.no, www.nordnet.dk or www.nordnet.fi.
